_comment: MOMO's baseline for the compare-youtube-channels skill. Niche is canonical per context/work.md (faceless ranking Shorts). Keep handles/metrics/named_competitors current as you learn them. YouTube channel stats are gated to scraping, so 'unknown' is honest — fill only what you can cite.

owned_channels.name: MOMO Shorts
owned_channels.note: primary channel — the rest of this profile describes it

owned_channels.name: moemen yasser
owned_channels.youtube: (handle unconfirmed — confirm from clipping-auto's token/channel next run)
owned_channels.niche: creator-clip Shorts (auto-clipped MrBeast videos, word-highlight captions)
owned_channels.note: benchmark against top clipping accounts, not the ranking-Shorts set

niche: Faceless ranking / Top-N list Shorts (entertainment, fast-paced)
posting_cadence: no fixed schedule — rank_autopost.py runs on-demand (manual trigger), not a cron/daily cadence (confirmed from projects/ranking shorts/ranking-shorts.md, 2026-07-01). This is itself a gap vs. the 10M-views/90-day YPP Shorts threshold, which effectively requires daily+ volume.

key_metrics.subscribers: unknown (small/new channel; YouTube stats gated to scraping)

known_weaknesses: Wants stronger hooks/retention, more viral picks, faster subscriber growth

named_competitors.name: FailArmy
named_competitors.note: Format benchmark for the fails genre; ruthless clip curation with the best moment front-loaded, not a Top-N countdown. 17.5M subs, ~4.96B total views, 981 videos, Shorts avg views ~87.2K, 2026 YTD uploads 71 Shorts vs 59 long-form (Social Blade / HypeAuditor / vidIQ, cited 2026-06-29).

named_competitors.name: America's Funniest Home Videos (AFV)
named_competitors.note: Premium clip library, clean captions, family-safe shareability. Main channel 7.84M subs (YouTube featured-channels data, cited 2026-06-29); runs a dedicated Shorts spinoff '@AFVHits' (AFV Shorts) at 177K subs -- worth noting as a possible model for a MOMO Shorts-only sub-brand.

named_competitors.name: WatchMojo
named_competitors.note: Closest large-channel format analog -- literal numbered Top-10 countdown, kinetic on-screen text, punchy pacing -- but it is long-form-first: 25.8M subs, 33.25K videos, 2026 YTD Shorts views 23.78M vs long-form 71.63M (258 Shorts vs 1.19K long-form uploads). Shorts are a minor channel, not the core product (vidIQ, cited 2026-06-29).

named_competitors.name: BRIGHT SIDE
named_competitors.note: 44.6M subs, list/curiosity-style long-form content; Shorts are negligible (1 upload YTD 2026) -- confirms even big curiosity/list channels haven't cracked Shorts-first. Not a format twin, included for completeness (vidIQ, cited 2026-06-29).

named_competitors.name: Polar Ranks
named_competitors.note: MOMO's actual scale peer and the best small-channel format match found so far -- viral-clip ranking/comparison Shorts (slap moments, fails, ankle breaks, crashes). UNVERIFIED/secondhand: ~587K subs, ~312M views/30-days, ~$1.2M revenue/30-days, ~2 years old, 'most videos crossing a million views' -- all stated inside a third-party niche-roundup video ("I Found 10 Hidden YouTube Shorts Niches for 2026" by @AImaskman, https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=A0Ea1cyRU98, cited 2026-06-29), not a primary analytics source. Re-verify via Social Blade/vidIQ directly next run before treating as solid.

named_competitors.name: Small/mid faceless Top-N countdown Shorts creators
named_competitors.note: MOMO's broader scale-peer tier -- same #N countdown, vertical-first, no brand moat; live/die on hook + topic. Repeated targeted web searches (2026-06-29) for 'faceless Top-N/countdown Shorts channel' surfaced almost entirely generic 'how to start a faceless channel' advice/listicle content, not specific citable handles -- this sub-tier isn't well-indexed by web search (Polar Ranks above is the one exception found). Re-attempt discovery next run via direct YouTube Shorts-shelf browsing rather than web search. Win them on cold-open teaser, kinetic reveals, and curiosity-gap topics.

goals: Make the ranking Shorts more viral and higher-quality, then expand to TikTok/Instagram. 'Exceeding' rivals = better hooks, higher retention, more views per Short, faster subscriber growth. Binding monetization constraint (confirmed 2026-07-01): YPP Shorts path needs 1,000 subs + 10M public Shorts views in a trailing 90 days (source: https://miraflow.ai/blog/youtube-shorts-watch-time-2026-how-much-you-need). Community consensus (r/NewTubers, anecdotal) is this is realistically hit via a small number of viral breakout Shorts, not steady low-view daily posting -- prioritize hook/topic virality and posting volume together, not either alone.
